text.heading: Kano State Government Aid Farmers in Boosting Food Production
text.p: The Kano State Government in an effort to boost the production of food in the State by its farmers has provided different species of seeds, including maize, cotton and groundnut, to ensure this year's farming season is more bountiful.

Disclosing this was the Alhaji Dahiru Abdu Garko who said the State Government is resolved to provide varieties of seedlings to farmers this year to address the outcry of insufficient food in the State.

He explained further that the programme to provide seedlings was also designed with a view to improving agricultural activities and promote farmers' efforts to harvesting larger quantity of food crops.

Alhaji Garko added that for the farmers to achieve the set objectives of the government, farmers' compliance with instructions given on conducting projects is paramount to ensure Kano State is free from the global food crisis.

According to him, should the farmers fail to abide by the given instruction, the State would likely experience a negative output in the agricultural sector which consequently would result in poor harvest and hunger.

However, progress reports have started to come in according to Alhaji Garko, indicating that the State agricultural ministry will succeed in its mission. He added that maize production in Gabasawa Local Government has been enhanced.

He then urged the trained farmers to endure the difficulty of the system, and in the meantime take advantage of the programme with good expectation and conduct according to the project's laid down procedure.

The progremme officer also hinted that anybody who purchases one bag of seeds and applies his acquired knowledge would unfailingly qualify to purchase fertilizers at the Ministry at an affordable price.
